title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Inject JavaScript into a forum with reflected and stored XSS vulnerabilities. Learn how user input rendered without sanitization can execute arbitrary scripts.

title: SQL Injection
description: Bypass authentication and extract data through SQL injection. The login form uses string concatenation to build SQL queries.

title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: Forge cross-site requests to transfer funds. The transfer form has no CSRF token protection.

title: Command Injection
description: Exploit a ping utility that passes user input directly to a shell command without sanitization.

title: Path Traversal
description: Escape the intended file directory and read sensitive server files using directory traversal sequences.

title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
description: Access other users' profiles by manipulating predictable object identifiers in the URL.

title: Broken Authentication
description: Forge session tokens to impersonate the admin. The session management uses predictable, easily reversible tokens.
